name: Vegetable Strudel (Bon Appetit/July 1983)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine broccoli, cauliflower, and carrots in a steamer.
2. Place over boiling water and steam until crisp-tender. Let cool slightly.
3. Melt butter in a medium skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until golden. Let cool slightly.
4. In a large bowl, combine eggs, parsley, and seasoning. Beat well. Add the steamed vegetables, onion mixture, and shredded cheese. Mix gently but thoroughly.
5. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
6. Oil a large baking sheet and set aside.
7. Place one phyllo sheet on a work surface (cover remaining phyllo with plastic wrap to prevent drying). Brush with melted butter.
8. Stack the remaining phyllo sheets on top, brushing all but the last sheet with butter. Spread the vegetable mixture onto the dough, leaving a 3-inch border on all sides.
9. Fold in the short ends of the dough, then fold in the long sides, overlapping at the center. Brush with butter to seal.
10. Carefully transfer the strudel to the prepared baking sheet. Brush the top with butter. Sprinkle with sesame seeds if desired.
11. Bake until golden, about 20 to 30 minutes. Transfer to a heated platter and serve immediately. Accompany with Hollandaise sauce.
